steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Both sides of the blank are independent clauses. The relationship is a contrast: the calculators were popular, but they were eventually replaced. A coordinating conjunction with a comma is required.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — comma alone (comma splice): two independent clauses cannot be joined by a comma without a conjunction. ✗
  • Choice B — no punctuation (fused sentence): running the clauses together with no separator is a fused sentence. ✗
  • Choice C — ', but': a comma plus the coordinating conjunction 'but' correctly joins two independent clauses and signals the contrast. ✓
  • Choice D — '; however,': a semicolon with a conjunctive adverb requires placing 'however' after the semicolon with a comma after it — the syntax here is ungrammatical as presented. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
